notes.old:Panchkula: A one-seat district before 2008: just Kalka, stretching from the Shivalik foothills to the Chandigarh periphery.
notes.new:Panchkula: Doubled to two seats — the new Panchkula seat recognises the planned city’s growth next to Chandigarh.
notes.old:Ambala: Six seats including Naggal, which vanished entirely in 2008. Sadhaura (SC) moved to Yamunanagar’s grouping.
notes.new:Ambala: Trimmed to four seats; Mullana’s spelling was modernised to Mulana.
notes.old:Karnal: Six seats; Jundla (SC) vanished and its reservation effectively moved to the new Nilokheri (SC).
notes.new:Karnal: Five seats; Assandh lost its SC reservation while Nilokheri gained one.
notes.old:Panipat: Three seats — Panipat, Sambhalka and Naultha; only the Panipat name survived, split into City and Rural.
notes.new:Panipat: Four seats, all effectively reorganised: Panipat City + Rural from the old Panipat, Israna (SC) from Naultha, Samalkha respelled from Sambhalka.
notes.old:Rohtak: Hassangarh vanished; Kiloi was renamed Garhi Sampla-Kiloi. This is the Jat heartland belt around Rohtak town.
notes.new:Rohtak: Four seats; Garhi Sampla-Kiloi is the renamed Kiloi.
notes.old:Sonipat: Six seats; Kailana and Rohat both vanished in the redraw.
notes.new:Sonipat: Six seats; Ganaur and Kharkhauda (SC) are new names, Sonepat was respelled Sonipat.
notes.old:Faridabad: Six seats covering today’s Faridabad and Palwal districts; Mewla Maharajpur vanished, seeding three new city seats.
notes.new:Faridabad: The biggest expansion in the state: nine seats, five of them new (Prithla, Faridabad NIT, Badkhal, Tigaon, Hodal SC) as the city boomed. Palwal district (2008) later took the southern seats.
notes.old:Gurgaon: Six seats including Taoru, which vanished into Nuh and Sohna.
notes.new:Gurgaon: Seven seats; Badshahpur and Punahana are new. Mewat district (2005) covers the Nuh–Punahana belt; the map source still groups them under Gurgaon.
notes.old:Hisar: Seven seats including Bhattu Kalan and Ghirai, both abolished in 2008.
notes.new:Hisar: Seven seats; Uklana (SC) and Nalwa are new names, Hissar was respelled Hisar.
notes.old:Sirsa: Five seats; Darba Kalan and Rori both vanished.
notes.new:Sirsa: Five seats; Kalanwali (SC) and Rania are new names. Ellenabad and Dabwali lost their SC reservations.
notes.old:Jind: Seven seats; Rajond vanished, its area anchoring the new Kalayat (shifted to Kaithal’s grouping).
notes.old:Kaithal: Four seats including Pai, which vanished into Pundri and the new Kalayat.
notes.new:Kaithal: Four seats; Kalayat moved here from Jind’s belt and lost its SC reservation.
notes.old:Rewari: Three seats; Jatusana vanished, its core becoming the new Kosli.
notes.old:Mahendragarh: Three seats in the state’s dry south-west corner.
notes.new:Mahendragarh: Four seats — Nangal Chaudhry was added from Narnaul and Ateli territory.
notes.old:Bhiwani: Seven seats; Mundhal Khurd vanished toward the new Nalwa (Hisar).
notes.new:Bhiwani: Six seats. Charkhi Dadri’s later district creation (2016) post-dates this map — Dadri and Badhra are shown here.
notes.old:Jhajjar: Five seats; Salhawas vanished into Jhajjar and Beri.
notes.old:Kurukshetra: Four seats; Shahabad was respelled Shahbad and became SC-reserved, Radaur lost its reservation.
notes.new:Kurukshetra: Four seats; Ladwa is a new name carved from Radaur and Thanesar.
notes.old:Fatehabad: Three seats, all continuing — the most stable grouping in the state.
notes.new:Fatehabad: Three continuing seats; Ratia stayed SC-reserved.
notes.old:Yamunanagar: Three seats; Chhachhrauli vanished into Jagadhri and Sadhaura.
notes.new:Yamunanagar: Four seats — gained Sadhaura (SC) and Radaur from neighbouring groupings.

sources: Delimitation of Parliamentary and Assembly Constituencies Order, 1976 & 2008; District-wise comparison of Haryana Assembly Constituencies Before and After the 2008 Delimitation; ECI Statistical Reports, 2005 & 2009 Haryana Assembly elections; ECI constituency polygons (2008); pre-delimitation all-India assembly-constituency shapefile.
method: Plate I draws the actual 1976-order boundaries from the pre-delimitation all-India assembly-constituency shapefile — all 90 Haryana seats present. Plate II draws the 2008 boundaries from the ECI's published polygons. Plate III sets both orders on one projection for direct comparison. Plate IV is schematic: tiles carry the record, not the geometry.

footnotes.t: Mewat (2005), Palwal (2008) and Charkhi Dadri (2016) districts post-date the map source; their seats are shown under Gurgaon, Faridabad and Bhiwani.

2005 winners are attached to the 1976-order seats and 2009 winners to the 2008-order seats, from the ECI statistical reports (2005: INC 67, INLD 9, BJP 2, BSP 1, NCP 1, IND 10 · 2009: INC 40, INLD 31, HJC(BL) 6, BJP 4, BSP 1, SAD 1, IND 7).
The Flipped/Held chips compare parties across the two elections only for seats whose name survived the redraw (continuing or renamed), traced by name or a single unambiguous parent.
